reviews.review_title: Not There Yet but the Cessna is Fun
reviews.review_description: I didn't have big expectations when purchasing the game. I knew it would be a work in progress. The game is definitely not a 1 star game for sure. It isn't 4 or 5 right now either. Both 1's and 5's are misleading reviews at this point. There are minimal cockpit instruments to control right now. The flaps are an issue. I tried toggling the flaps to get different degrees and it either went no flaps or full. I jumped in the f18 and it just kept crashing as soon as it loads. Flying the plane actually is fun and the yoke is fine with adjusting the sensitivity  to your liking. Trim controls are difficult because you have to use the tablet. Every time I adjust the trim the throttle goes back to full which is frustrating. I am sure a lot of this will get worked out but I hope this company isn't just relying on the community to find bugs. I hope they have testers noticing these issues as well. I did notice some sky tear at Moffet and some weird shadowing at Everest. Overall it is middle of the road right now. I am a developer and I think I would have just focused on the Cessna first. Getting all the instrumentation working and the flight engine up to speed. I think then it would have been easier to build the next plane and so on. I do like it though.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review.  I've responded to your points below.
- The trim issue with the throttle/tablet has been fixed, the update will release tomorrow.
- The flaps can be adjusted marginally in cockpit, we'll add this to the controller input as well
- F18 crashing - can you please email us and let us know where this is happening? mail@tasappsystems.com 
- We weren't planning to model all of the switches in the cockpits (ie: startup procedures, fuel mixtures, etc.).  It's very difficult to handle in VR for casual players.  However, this has been requested in multiple reviews and the feature has been added to our "to-do" list. We plan to model the main startup procedure, add fuel mixture controls, as well as in cockpit trim wheels.  If there's anything else you think is critical to have, please email us: mail@tasappsystems.com 

Thanks for your support.

reviews.review_description: As someone who plays msfs 24 from time to time I thought I'd give it a try it's really good I'm impressed with the developers work so far and I'm looking forward to the game updates it needs more locations but I'm sure they will come with time so far so good I'll update my review as time goes by.

developer_response.body:
6/18/26 - A big update just released that adds numerous controls to the Cessna.

Thanks for the comments. Yes, we have big updates in the works. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Flight unlimited is a title with a proud history. This seems like a big letdown of that legacy.
Even in pre-release and on special offer I paid £15.

The good:
Satellite imagery for the scenery
Half decent stall behaviour

The bad:
World scale for the plane is off (planes feel too big)
No control via joysticks of controllers or external control device.
The hands-on-virtual-yoke control is so bad that most of my time was spent looking at the yoke to ensure no random erratic control inputs.
The visuals for the plane’s interior and exterior (as far as visible from the cockpit window) are disappointing.
No fuel tank selection and conflicting fuel gauge information.
No engine management (mixture settings etc)

In comparing with “Simple planes” and “Dawn of jets” I can see what the Quest3 is capable of. This title falls far short and £15 is too big a gamble on “they might just pull it off someday”.

developer_response.body: We’re sorry you’re dissapointed. This is early access. The items you mentioned will be added before full release. Regarding the other titles you mentioned, our scenery is pushing 10x the polys and texture data. It’s a major feat that we’ve been able to push this level of detail on a mobile processor. Regardless, the game will continue to see major updates over the coming weeks and months. We’d appreciate it if you can update your review as the game improves and progresses. Thank you

reviews.review_description: Considering the hardware limitations- I think the developers have done an outstanding job putting a sim (that functions well and looks great) on a device with a fraction of the power a standalone sim historically requires. Looking forward to future development, aircraft and locations. Very satisfied. (I am a licensed pilot fwiw)

Love the feeling of height u get when navigating Everest .. wonderful .... 

It's getting better 😁


Some glimmers of greatness most definitely & huge amounts of potential 

Sone really unfair first reviews ppl don't seem to understand it's a work in progress not a finished product 

Banking sharply over the grand canyon in a Cessna truly was breathtaking ..😎

Can't wait to see u build on it 

I honestly believe this is gonna be the benchmark for all genuine standalone/native  flight Sims ..

Joystick haptics needed  and reflections (looks like some work has been done the windows in latest update march 5?) on windows /surfaces etc for much deeper immersion 

Again cheers 🥂

***In the April 16th update did u remove the windscreen effect on all aircraft ? It was a great addition & made u feel as if u were in the plane without it really impacts immersion (the feeling of being inside safe etc)..other than that stability is hugely improved & love the ils ..hope we get the windows back ..its truly needed 

Great news & really starting to look fantastic ..the stutter is almost gone ..exciting times .. gr8 work all 🫡👏

Need the windscreens back ..it's essential for immersion .. essential !!  Night time ...turbulence ..etc etc cmon guys  u can do this u are soooooo close do doing something never done on a standalone 

Update** much more immersive with all the additional steps for the Cessna .. ** slow going but well worth the time it takes  can't wait (but happy to😊) for what's coming in the future & apologies for my impatience 



👊👊🔥🔥🔥


Cheers

developer_response.body:
Thank you again for your support!
No need to apologize, sorry it’s taking so long.
Rest assured, we’re working around the clock.
This was a very extensive update due to the functions 
of each control. Updates will be released on a shorter
time frame from here on out.

reviews.review_description:
I was a little concerned after reading some of the reviews, but I was pleasantly surprised by how good this actually is.

There are around 12 aircraft already available, with more on the way, ranging from small Cessnas to the F-18. While they all use the same basic flight controls, but they each look and fly very differently, which keeps things fresh and interesting.

There are several airports to choose from where you can practise circuits and free flying, plus a handful of activities to try, with more content reportedly on the way. I’m really looking forward to the upcoming weather updates.

My only wish would be for the terrain was a little more 3D in places and for there to be moving traffic on the roads below, although that may simply be pushing the limits of the native Quest 3 hardware.

Considering this is still in early access, the developers seem very active with patches and updates, which is great to see. I’m genuinely excited to see where this game goes in the future.

If you’re considering this as a flight simulator, it’s important to set expectations. While there’s a decent selection of aircraft, the systems and controls are still EXTREMELY basic, so it currently feels much more like an arcade flying experience than a true sim.
That’s where some disappointment comes in. The foundation is promising, but it doesn’t yet offer the procedural depth or realism someone might expect if they’re looking to practice real-world flying.
What makes this especially noticeable is the missed opportunity. There’s nothing else on standalone VR that really targets student pilots. If the focus shifted toward just one or two trainer aircraft with accurate systems, startup procedures, and flight behavior, this could become something genuinely valuable rather than just another casual flying game.
As it stands, it’s enjoyable for what it is, but if you’re expecting a realistic simulator, you may want to wait and see how it develops.

developer_response.body: Big news! The latest update (released today) adds full controls and startup procedure to the Cessna! The other planes will follow in the coming weeks!

reviews.review_description:
It’s been out a while and it’s in early access, so some of the reviews seem unfair, I couldn’t wait to jump into the Boeing aeroplane, the sounds are just amazing & spot on.
I hope some sharper graphics come, I know the dev has been working hard on updates, with more still to come, so the game has so much more in the pipeline.
Very excited to where the game is going, any chance of some mission based journeys in the near future.
For now I like what the dev is doing.

Had big expectations for this but it's not fulfilling those. Every update brings the same bugs. The 747 looks clunky and amateur. I'm thinking of returning to world flight - my previous flight sim unless things change. So far £23.99 is a big waste of my hard earned money!

developer_response.body:
We’re not sure which bugs you’re referring to.
We’re making significant updates.
We’ve added ILS instrumentation, the G1000, to appropriate cockpits, improved flight models, etc. 
This is also just the beginning. We have countless updates in the works. It would be helpful if you can email us the bugs you’re referring to. Thanks! mail@tasappsystems.com

reviews.review_description:
So far, considering it's an early access it isn't too bad but my main complaint I have is the lack of locations.
If there was more countries that you could fly within I would enjoy the game a lot more than I currently do, like previously stated, It isn't too bad still

it's a decently fun simulator buthe would like to be able to use a controls and what also like to have some missions. also when it comes to the graphics i do notice that there's some sort of a strange halo around my hands that i do not see with other flight simulators on meta quest  3. i look forward to seeing how this game develops in the future

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comments. Additional controls are in the works, as are missions. This is an early access title, it’s not complete yet. We’d appreciate it if you can update your review as the game improves and grows

reviews.review_description:
WOW! CONGRATULATIONS to the developers! I am an FAA-licensed IFR pilot. I wanted to "fly virtually" when I couldn't fly in reality.  I was very frustrated searching for a stand-alone Q3 flight sim app that actually felt like flying a real airplane with a working instrument panel. And buying a $2K-$3K "gaming PC" just to run MSFS 2024 was a non-starter.

This is NOT a "combat" or "scenery-flying" game, but one that gives you realistic flying feel. That's what I was primarily looking for. I can play "bang-bang shoot-em-up" games elsewhere.

DEVELOPERS: Please stay focused on tuning the "pilot experience" of all the REAL skills like accurate handling & physics, shooting IFR approaches and doing "touch-and-gos". Leave the pretty 3D scenery to a lower priority.

SUGGESTIONS: It would be great to have a "pop-up" type set of  reminders: 1) how to interact with ALL the instruments for each aircraft; 2) reposition the cockpit to fit my chair position; 3) re-mappable controller buttons. Consider adding: a) using the hand-controller "twist" action to control the rudder; b) set up approaches in a Flight Director; c) a "glass cockpit" version on some aircraft (like the Cirrus SR22T). Please focus on building all the features nto your app that make MSFS 2024 a realistic "pilot experience" and not worry about pretty scenery. Realism, controls, configurability, interactability.

KEEP GOING! You have my attention and support! You're off to a great start. I just hope you can sustain your momentum.

PS-I've read ALL the reviews thus far, and most of them were expecting MSFS-like sophistication and scenery right off the bat. For my money, focus on the feedback that tells you about how to improve the "pilot experience". I would challenge those reviewers to find ANYTHING CLOSE to this app on a stand-alone Q3 flight sim. No cables, no subscriptions, no expensive PC. Just pure flight joy!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to write these comments. This is extremely helpful. We’d love to ask you a few questions so we can plan our next updates. Your feedback is extremely valuable. If you have time, can you please reach out to us via email: mail@tasappsystems.com 

Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
Excellent. Premium champagne grade awesome. Exceeds expectations. Spectacular. Profound. 

Photorealistic scenes from Google maps. Mountains are totpographically accurate and to scale.

All airplanes are very beautiful and breathtakingly elegant. This is a million dollar app. Top class!

F18. Bait and switch. Future faker. Supposedly to be released on December 18, Then January 19, Then February 26, then March 26, Now April 16th. Supposedly. I'll believe when I see it.
Is this how you run a business?
I'm thinking of contacting Anne Drewa of Global News. Consumer Matters.
Shoddy business practice. Who taught you how to run a business?!


April 2026 - Flight Unlimited. In this case, Unlimited means 4  of the planes not being available.
Which leaves me wondering what the app would have looked like if it was called Flight Ltd. 
4 of the planes still not available 8 months after the app was released. That's a two months per plane delay and counting. What do you call that? Not bad! Non-plussing.

Knowledge is unlimited. Life is limited. If you use the limited to pursue the unlimited, you will lose."  Confucius

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review. 
We’ve been focused on flight model improvements and most importantly, adding the G1000 to the cockpits, as well as ILS navigation.  These are major undertakings.

Next we’re adding map functionality, and will unlock another plane in the next few weeks.

reviews.review_description:
When is the next update.
Did you guys give up on this game?
This game would be great if you stay on top of it.

developer_response.body:
We’re hard at work developing updates. 
The next update is a big one, that’s why it’s taking a while. 
It will add more controls and the startup procedure to the Cessna. The other planes will follow after. The update is scheduled to release next week.

***6-18-26: big new update just released!

reviews.review_description:
There's a field cut on the left-hand side of my right eye view, which is incredibly distracting. It renders the game unplayable for me. Please fix this!

:Update:

Thanks to a very responsive developer, the issue has been resolved. Added another star, and I expect I'll add another down the line as this app continues to develop!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the update!

reviews.review_description:
I bought it and played it a little - it promises to become an excelent game. I hope it will have missions, training and maybe take off and landing guidance and many other features. When do you estimate it will be finished? Thank you and keep up the good work!
Update: any news?….

I spent most of yesterday with this app. I couldn't get away from it. Flying the Cessna 172 was just too much fun. I spent a lot of hours in one of these for work in the 70's. I am very familiar with the handling. This is very close to the real thing. And for those of you that have spent time with Microsoft's Flight Simulator, this has got what MSFS has always been missing, a sense of depth. I can line up on a runway and put the plane down without needing the assistance of ILS because I can now tell where I am in relation to the ground. Kudos to the developers. Please keep up the good work. I truly hope this is going to keep expanding. I look forward to, hopefully, cross country flights to other airports and the addition of more advanced planes and avionics.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the comments! We’re thrilled to have such positive feedback from a pilot. The next big update will feature ILS landings via an in cockpit G1000. We expect to have this update ready in 2-3 weeks.

reviews.review_description:
Dear developers you promised us with a flight simulator for quest3 ,
I'm waiting for the full ✋️ hands control  from start the engine to landing, 
Also the graphics is a lot of glitches actually there is a lot comments about the app I think you already know. 
But please 🙏 tell us when. It's still paralyzed and when you try to choose a destination it increases the throttle developers should try it more it makes you dizzy on another hand your other apps are all good 👍 wish you good luck please keep on updating it. 
With all respect and appreciation

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the updated comments. 
We’re listening, and will have these things updated in 
the near future.

Update: 
Big news! The latest update (released today) adds full controls and startup procedure to the Cessna! The other planes will follow in the coming weeks!

reviews.review_description: I've had this game now for about 4 months they ain't going to  to it still the same it ain't no good you can't do nothing with it I went by it again but that way

developer_response.body:
We’ve released over 25 updates. 
We’ve added full gps, added multiple new aircraft, added ILS instrumentation, improved performance significantly, upgraded the flight model significantly, added an in cockpit tablet with full data reports and trim controls. We’re currently working on the Cessna startup procedure controls, Flight school missions, and a whole lot more.

Your review mentions none of the significant updates the game is receiving week after week.

reviews.review_title: THE TRUE FLIGHT SIMULATOR FOR META QUEST
reviews.review_description:
Flight Unlimited is currently the most realistic flight simulator for Meta Quest Standalone. Considering the hardware limitations of the Meta Quest 2 and 3, the developers have done an excellent job: the simulator is highly enjoyable even in its current Early Access stage.

Suggestion for the developers
It would be fantastic if the simpler aircraft, such as the Cessna 172, featured fully functional flight instruments, including the ability to use COM/NAV radios and VOR navigation.

If Flight Unlimited receives consistent updates with new features and scenarios, it has the potential to become the gold standard for flight simulation on Meta Quest Standalone.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the comments. VOR navigation will be available in an update releasing in 2-3 weeks. We’re also working on additional instruments. Please check back often

reviews.review_description:
Update:   you now have a map and I can land a 747.  Cool.  Thanks


Graphics are great.  The contols are a bit wild and dont respond.   Im sure more updates are coming.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for the comments.
Regarding the controls. Can you please email 
us some details? mail@ tasappsystems.com

reviews.review_description: I Play casually on MSFS, but that game needs a monster PC and takes time to set up to play in VR. This one though... I see it going to be like Aerofly FS 4 but in native quest hardware. The Devs should be talking to IPACS haha!. Keep on Developing this game, don't stop and quit. Keep up the good work! Keep pushing the limits of quest and hopefully this game becomes a go to flight sim for Quest owners.

developer_response.body: Thank you, we’re just getting started!

reviews.review_title: Feels realistic when flying high enough
reviews.review_description: Nice sceneries, nice flying physics. In my opinion, improve the graphics. I think what I do is to sightsee in such a game. Add labels to interesting points, so that I know what I am seeing. Add the possibility to be able to navigate to the Mount Everest which I don’t know what it looks like, even when I stand in front of it.
A very ambitious VR flight simulator already showing huge potential in Early Access. Beautiful scenery, detailed cockpits and great immersion for aviation fans!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support! We're just getting started.  Since Flight Unlimited became available for early access we've updated the map system, added the G1000 to appropriate cockpits, added ILS systems for instrument approaches, improved flight physics, etc.  The next few updates will be massive!  Check back often.

reviews.review_title: The lier type jet has a hard time sterli
reviews.review_description: Unstable when landing the jets run out of runway because you have speed that needs to be reversed after landed to help stop the plane

developer_response.body:
Thanks for pointing this out, we’ll work on a fix. 
Potential reverse thrust and/or stronger brakes

reviews.review_description: Not a big selection of planes no autopilot and there’s no gps to help us locate airports and our position make the maps bigger more planes like commercial jets and private jets and military aircraft yet again I like the game it’s very fun but not enough content for 30$ also be able to mod in this game it’ll get you a lot more buyers and we can get a lot of planes without u working on them

developer_response.body: This is early access. We’re releasing multiple updates in the coming weeks that adds GPS, additional aircraft, etc. Please check back and revise your review once the updates launch

reviews.review_description:
To start, there have to be some caveats…
One, this is an early release so there will be occasional bugs and glitches. 
Two, this is a stand-alone on a Quest headset. I think too many people forget that the Quest has the computing power of a smartphone and often try to compare games/apps to a gaming PC.

Now with the above understood, I think you would struggle not to be impressed with the work that has gone into bringing something like this to a stand-alone headset. Is it a full blown simulation, no but it’s probably the nearest you are going to get without linking to a PC. The flight physics aren’t too bad and I’m sure they’ll be ‘tweaked’ as the game grows and patches released.

To the Dev’s - a couple of things I would like to see if possible…
1. The ability to be able trim the pitch.
2. Sound is never going to be accurate on the Quest. Is there any ability or computing power left to squeeze in some ATC chatter, even just some random looped background ‘noise’. This would then distract from the engine sounds slightly and improve the experience, and to some extend sense of realism.
3. To figure out landing speeds, I tried to stall the aircraft so I could work out a good approach and landing speeds. I’m struggling to stall some of the aircraft !! The 747 in particular, even with the throttles closed and speed brake raised, she just kept ‘flying’ !! Just an observation more than anything….

Keep up the good work and I’m looking forward to see how this ‘sim’ evolves 😃

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the comments! Our replies are below:

1. You can adjust the trim pitch on the tablet in the cockpit
2. Yes, we're working on adding ATC chatter, this should be updated within 3-4 weeks if all goes well.
3. Thanks for pointing this out, this shouldn't be happening.  We'll take a closer look at the flight model and see where it's going wrong.  

If you have any more comments please feel free to reach out: 
mail@tasappsystems.com

Update: 
Big news! The latest update (released today) adds full controls and startup procedure to the Cessna! The other planes will follow in the coming weeks!

reviews.review_description: The game is great, planes are awesome but I have noticed the Cessna 172 is very hard to control, when making a steep turn the yoke flips to the other side turning you the other direction causing you to crash.  I would love to see mixture and carb heat put into play as something you control while in the Cessna as well as the flaps.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comments. The Cessna shouldn’t be doing that. We’ll look into it

reviews.review_title: Not the worst, but close to it
reviews.review_description:
The visuals are okay. The flight mechanics are manageable but need a lot of work. As for the claim of being a PC worthy flight simulator…that is complete nonsense! There are only four flight components you get to control. No interactive buttons. And the flaps are only up or down, nothing imbetween.
If I could claim my money back I would. There are way better flight simulators for the Quest VR already. Also a few worse ones though 🤗

developer_response.body:
Please stay tuned for updates. 
This is early access. We have significant updates being worked on. As for additional buttons, please email us with what you’d like to see: mail@tasappsystems.com

reviews.review_title: When are the other planes coming out?
reviews.review_description: The game is great not gonna lie but I just feel like it’s an incomplete game. It’s had a small update recently booked. They haven’t gave us any of the other planes if any of the people who made this game see this please reply to this comments and tell me when they are coming out, please because I love this game but it needs a new content

developer_response.body: The other planes are on hold while we finish our navigation update. We have the C130 ready to release, as well as improvements to other aircraft. We just can’t release the update until our navigation update is ready (due to coding complexities). We hope to release part 1 of 2 of navigation on Friday, along with the C130

reviews.review_description:
To all the user's that were expecting perfection may I remind you this is early access on a stand alone headset yes its buggy and takes some time to get use to but I am sure the developer is hard at work on updates and patches I recommend this based on the fact the sheer scale of this project and buy this if your willing to wait for patches if not buy a gtx 5020 and microsoft flight simulator vr then you might appreciate the hard work that has gone in to this project


However dear developer I do have one small request which would make this game shear perfection for me even in early access is the bell helicopter  I would happily pay any price for this hope you read this and keep up the good work look forward to future updates

developer_response.body: Thank you, yes, we're working tirelessly and will have countless updates over the coming weeks and months

reviews.review_description:
I'm a former MSFS addict and have been looking for a good VR flight sim that would work well on my headset without connecting to a computer for a long time. This one is extremely promising! It's still in development, but so far it's got me excited. The graphics are excellent compared to other VR flight sims I've seen so far, and the flight models feel good - I'm not constantly fighting to control the planes. Can't wait for more functionality with the cockpit controls and navigation, so that I can navigate to airports and perform ILS approaches/landings. The most encouraging part is that the developers are making regular improvements and I'm not having to wait for years. A couple of other promising sims just went radio-silent with regard to updates.

Side note: It's probably me, but I run out of fuel way too fast with the business jet and the fighter jet. The fuel burn can't possibly be that fast! 😅

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the comments and thanks for pointing out the fuel burn issue. We’ll look into that next week.

This next update is one of our longer ones, which is why we haven’t released an update in a couple weeks. It’s a great update though, the G1000 with navigation will be in the applicable aircraft. We’re on track to release it in 1-2 weeks

reviews.review_description: I'm sorry but I'm really not impressed with this simulator, it's slow to react when trying to control and so unrealistic.

developer_response.body: Sorry you’re not impressed. The control is actually very realistic and based on advanced physics principles. We’re constantly making updates and improvements as well

reviews.review_description:
In a word... yes.

I used to fly Cessna 172 until my health fell apart and could no longer pass my medical. That was over 25 years ago. Flight Unlimited has allowed me to have the "sense" of flying again. My son gifted me his Quest 2. He bought a Quest 3 or something. 

In September I underwent a below knee amputation and since my purchase I've enjoyed the game... to an extent.

She's buggy. Really buggy but it is "early access" so maybe patience is truly a virtue. Running up the Pitts, Robin, and P-40 saw me flip nose down into the ground. The scenery is okay once you reach altitude. As far as the Challenger goes... I think my plane and all aboard vanished over the Pacific. It went all kaput.

There's potential here but is there the patience to see it out.

I've debated trying to get my money back. For the record my son did get a refund. He likes the idea of physically pushing the buttons.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support. Can you please email us with more details on the on the "flip nose down" issue?  We'd like to recreate it so we can see what's going on under the hood.  If you can email us at mail@tasappsystems.com that would be great, thanks!

reviews.review_description: I really like this game, it is worth every penny. The cockpit is so detailed and I like the way that you can do different missions like flying over muntevrest

developer_response.body: Thank you! The game will receive numerous updates in the coming weeks

reviews.review_title: I have been waiting for this
reviews.review_description: If this is the beta drop, I can’t wait to see more, 100% LOVE! I have been waiting to sit at the controls of an airliner and fly. I know this isn’t prefect, but as starts go it’s not far off. Wants, more buttons, maybe a check list for the big birds? Needs, better placement of the iPad, and gages. Will I keep playing, YES! Can’t wait to start putting in the hours to learn how to fly in your world.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the comments.  We have significant updates scheduled over the coming months...including additional controls, tutorials, instrument landing guides, etc. 

Btw, you can grab the iPad and move it wherever you want.

reviews.review_title: Learn what`s Early Access before buying
reviews.review_description:
The game is not perfect, of course. It is running on Quest 3 and even Quest 2, which is very limited hardware, and it is still in Early Access. That being said, you can clearly see that the dev team has put a lot of effort into it and has already done a solid job.

What really annoys me is seeing people complain about an Early Access game. First, they complain that there are not many options on Quest. Then, when a new game actually shows up with an Early Access tag, everyone starts criticizing it instead of understanding what Early Access means.

Flight sims, especially the more hardcore ones, are a very niche genre. The studio could easily be making another generic MMO and selling skins, but instead they decided to build a flight simulator. Comparing this to a Microsoft flight sim running on a monster PC with a high-end GPU makes no sense. That kind of overly picky feedback just pushes developers toward making more and more dumbed-down games.

I trust the dev team and believe the game will keep improving with updates and fixes. I am keeping the game, supporting the project, and I want to congratulate the developers for what they have achieved so far.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the comments. We’re aware of the issues and are working  on updates and fixes. This game will be fully supported until content is added and all issues are fixed. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
Vision is there but in no way is this ready! Control interaction, or lack of I should say, graphics, and pop in is so terrible. Runs terrible!
  It Is Not, I repeat, NOT the best looking sim Or most interactive in the quest store By Far! I’ve tried everything to get this to run at least semi smoothly and still it is so laggy and jittery. Five star reviews are a joke or they haven’t tried any of the other polished games that got it right before releasing. 
 I want to love this sim, it’s close to being cool, but at this point right now there are just too many issues that break the immersion, The ground disappearing constantly for one lol, so I simply can not recommend it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for sticking with it, we’re updating the game often. Regarding pop-in. Please remember the Quest is mobile hardware, the pop in is the only way we can achieve the required 72fps. We continue to optimize but we’re using every trick and optimization technique possible, including ASW. Our visuals are significantly better than any other flight game on the Quest. Regardless, we’re still working tirelessly to improve the game

reviews.review_description:
Terrible game. Environment and scenery are awful. Usability awlful. Waste of money. Can I get a refund?
I lasted about 30mins of frustration and being
 underwhelmed and won't go on it again. There are better flying games on the quest. I will give flying on the pc a go.

developer_response.body: Sorry you're not liking the game.  Please remember this is early access and a huge undertaking.  We are working around the clock on improvements.  We have spent over 2 years developing a way to get top notch scenery on the Quest.  Yes, you can ask Meta for a refund.

reviews.review_description: I have flight experience with ultralights and gliders- This game is at a fantastic start and im checking every day for an update and am looking forward to using it as my primary flight simulator when I am home.  I am happy to help in any way I can to keep this game progressing into the future! thanks for creating this exciting simulator :D

developer_response.body: Thanks for your continued support. The next couple weeks will be an exciting one for updates!

reviews.review_description: Wanted to return it, but was on the 15th day trying it for the second time. Difficult to control the plane, and pretty poor graphics. I play xplane & msfs on pc so was expecting something similar graphics wise.

developer_response.body: We’re updating the game constantly. Please stay tuned. Also, this is running on mobile hardware with a 70fps requirement. Xplane or MSFS are running on hardware 20x faster. Regardless, we’ll continue to optimize and update. We’re hoping you’ll change your 1 star rating when you’re more satisfied with the updates

reviews.review_description:
I mean no disrespect to the developers who I'm sure are hard at work, and they're building things I could never build. However, I'm the paying customer so I think my opinion is still valid. 

This game is not close to "AAA flight simulation physics", and I think it's borderline disingenuous to say it is. It's probably a $4.99 game maybe. The three planes I tried piloting were basically un-flyable. I've have a decent amount of experience with PC flight sims, as well as flight sims in the Quest, plus I've flown a few times IRL, so I'm not a complete novice. I couldn't keep the plane at level flight, it would wildly pitch up and down, and then it would go full nose up even if I was pushing the stick down - regardless of what I did with the throttle. None of the buttons work. Most of the dials don't work, they're just images. If you do something with the controller - like change the flaps - it's not reflected in the cockpit, so you have no idea whether the flaps are up or down. There's nothing to do in the game except fly around, and the scenery is one of those where its completely flat, so it looks decent when you're in the air but as soon as you get close to landing, it's bad. 

Sorry guys. Please do keep working on it, and I'll come back in a year or two and see where you're at. I wish you the best of luck.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for the comments...our flight model takes everything into account included air pressure on all surfaces.  We do have some bugs with a few of the aircraft which we're working on.

Can you please email us with a list of the issues you experienced and which aircraft?
We're working around the clock on fixing any characteristics that aren't working correctly.

mail@tasappsystems.com

reviews.review_description:
I bought this expecting that it will continue to improve through frequent updates and become more realistic over time (within its limits, of course).

So this is an optimistic 5-star rating, based mostly on expectation rather than solid proof.
That said, I am already having some fun with it.

I’m looking forward to seeing how it evolves, and I’m rooting for the developers.



I also hope to see Japanese language support in the future.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your support, we're releasing updates frequently. We're on track for at least one a week depending on complexity.

reviews.review_description: I don't think that this simulation is worth your money. It has bad graphics and has slow rendering. It sometimes does not register steering movements. I would recommend VMF instead of this simulation.

developer_response.body: Are you using a Quest 2 or 3? It’s optimized for quest 3. The mobile processor isn’t capable of more graphically. However, this is early access and is being updated

reviews.review_description:
Ok ok ok, let’s write this a bit better! 

I did not see what I was getting from the trailer in the game, I know it’s early access so maybe the price should reflect this. 

I just feel it’s was very laggy and had no control of the plane, also the graphic were not great at all.  

I can see the vision for this game so I’ll keep it and won’t refund it! And just pray something good happens.

developer_response.body: Sorry you're not liking the game.  Please remember this is early access and a huge undertaking.  We are working around the clock on improvements

reviews.review_description:
* Work in Progress review *

There’s a big variety of aircraft in this flight sim lite game… with more to be added. I think some of the aircraft size scales in the hangar and from the cockpit, seem a little off… as I know some of the aircraft are bigger that what they are portrayed as in the hangar. And some cockpits/engine ect.. seem a little too long. 
The interiors of each aircraft are nice. Maybe add some helicopters?

The sounds are just ok… engine sound seems the same for each aircraft though which is odd. But there’s not much more sounds, the odd cockpit instrument warnings and voices.

The controls to me felt a little sluggish but adjusting the settings helped improve that… so maybe it’s just personal preference with the basic set up for me needing adjusting.
There is very basic controls as there isn’t much you can do with handheld controllers, which helps to keep it accessible to anyone as you don’t need to have much flight sim experience to be able to play and enjoy.

The maps are ok… look better from a higher altitude though.

It’s a work in progress, so I expect it to keep improving and getting more added as it goes along.

Definitely recommended for anyone who enjoys flight simulators, or just aviation, the controls are intuitive, there are plenty of airports to choose from and aircraft to select. its not perfect, looking forward to the fine tuning,   I understand there are some updates to come so definitely worth checking out.  One for the library for sure.

I use to take flaying lessons many moons ago.  Loved the way this is going and so much more potential. Of course graphics are not on the same lines as MSFS 2024  but it will do. Minus the 2grand rig . Hey it’s oculus.   My only complaint is that when you break of release break after a landing .if you press tail rudder the plane wing dips and you crash. . And may add some VASI lights  .this would help when on approach on a glide slope. Thanks 🙏🏾

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the comments, we just submitted a new update that unlocks the E2C Hawkeye and fixes a few bugs.  

The next major update will be the G1000 (Garmin) for visualization and flight planning.  

VASI Lights are a great idea!

reviews.review_description: Great game but could use some more stuff its fun for the first 3 hours then get mad boring need so missons like transporting NPCs make there  flights that take 20+ mins transporting npcs

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comments. This is a great idea and we’ll be adding things like this in the near future…before the full release

reviews.review_description: I like the concept and I see some potential but as of now, the planes are hard to control, the cockpit controls aren’t user friendly, I find myself focusing more on the controls and playing with them before they take affect. There should be something to acknowledge that the system is active. My biggest wish is to being able to navigate from point a to point b and more options of places to fly. I wish that more controls would work. The more the better. What about some training before flying???  Pretty good app so far, and if these wishes of mine came true the it’s a 5 star from me.

This simulator is brilliant it's a good start to what appears to be really interesting, there's a lot to learn about flying you can't just jump in an airplane and expect to fly it it takes hours of practice. I'm looking forward to the updates  but up to now I'm glad i got it, carry on and get the improvements carried out, can't wait.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the comments, we’re working endlessly to improve the game. Please email with any specific requests

reviews.review_title: Has potential but not there yet
reviews.review_description:
Some of the aircraft are kinda fun, others are a buggy mess. But I appreciate what the devs are trying to do so hopefully they can keep improving it. I’m lookin forward to the Grumman Goose and hope it comes with a Virgin Island base LoL

Edit: Does this game have a Discord or some other social where we can track the update    progress?

Edit: So the dev keeps promising that “Big improvements are coming” but so far all we’ve seen is window dressing and the game is still unplayable. I just lowered my rating from 3 stars down to two. Fix your game, we payed money for this junk.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE - Please take a look at the latest build.  We have improved the aircraft behaviors and included trim/sensitivity controls, as well as raw data output of the forces on the plane.

**old comment**
We are updating the game and working around the clock. Please understand it takes time. The trim and sensitivity controls will be out next week, along with substantial behavioral improvements for each aircraft.

reviews.review_description:
I purchased the game, after
 playing for about 10 mins I was
 done and wanted a refund. But I
 going to wait!, mainly because I love flying games and this looks to be a good one once development is completed. 

Don't let me down guys!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your support. We have countless updates to come, including a full fledged aircraft creator/editor, missions, more buildings, more aircraft, etc

reviews.review_description: This simulation is very promising, even if on the cockpit you can phisically manipulate only the yoke, gear, aerobrake, thrust and flaps, but I count on the developers they will add more interactivity. BUG: when you click on the onboard tablet to adjust pitch trim, for example, you have to use laser pointer to click with the right or left controller trigger button, and this increase or decrease also the thrust because this is the predefined mapping! Please correct this! And keep up the good work!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your comments.
We’re updating the cockpits with more controls as well as navigation. We’re also aware of the bug with the trim/sensitivity. This will be fixed in a few days

reviews.review_description:
Gameplay is genuinely interesting. It has a real “sim” feel—finally not another cardboard-box-with-wings experience like a lot of what we’ve had on Quest.

Huge credit to the devs: what’s here is promising and I’m glad it exists. That said, even for early access, it feels a bit too early in its current state.

My recommendation: double down on the flight-sim feel and solid flight scenarios, and deprioritize “coming soon” features, visuals, hand tracking, and scenery polishing for now.

What’s critical for this game to succeed:

- Speed and navigation must be available, clear, and always visible.
- Add landing-start scenarios (start in the air on approach, not only from the ground).
- Add replay, especially a runway/tower view replay.
- These core features are what create replayability—so we can fly over and over and over.

Right now the vision feels split. Either commit to being a flight sim with the core elements that make it a sim (and attract people who want a realistic experience), or make it a lighter “for fun” flying game with hand tracking and scenery. Trying to be both usually weakens the end result—pick a direction and execute it well.

Have you played Extreme Landings on Android? That’s the kind of replayable scenario-based sim structure this could really benefit from.

If I want something where I just fake-fly and look around the world, there are already tons of options. What you’ve put together here is already fantastic at its core—please don’t lose that by chasing visuals too early. Reduce graphics if needed, lock in the fundamentals, and build the best VR flight sim you can first. The people who want this will come for the sim experience, not prettier views.

developer_response.body: This is great info! Thanks for the recommendations, very helpful! We agree with your assessment. Stay tuned for many many updates to come

reviews.review_description:
It is impossible to handle planes. 
It is not smooth, graphics are ugly and flight models are kit accurate 

Impossible to handle the plane to do a landing. Great idea but should be removed from the store.

developer_response.body: Sorry you’re having trouble flying. We’re not receiving other reports. If you need help please email us.

reviews.review_title: Basic, not ready, even for early release
reviews.review_description:
Initially I was like wow a 747 from the 1980s this is amazing! But then realised that functions are limited (flaps, brakes, stick and that's pretty much it) and the rest of the dials are just show and don't do anything.  

I'm not expecting full flight sim but would expect it not to have a meltdown when I try to extend/retract gear and basically just plummit me to the floor.

I managed to do loops on the 747 which was fun, if unrealistic. 

Another occasion I managed to land my plane semi underneath the runway and get wedged in between the tarmac and whatever is underneath. 

Not a lot to do apart from fly around over what looks like Google earth (i.e flat pics of buildings). I tried going to altitude and noticed that after a short while you can't see the earth any more and instead you're just in clouds which are basically just multiple cut/paste pics of clouds. No depth.  Thought jumping in an F18 and spanking it up to altitude would be fun, but after 60 seconds of heading up I can't see anything apart from the cut/paste clouds and it doesn't matter how high you go the view is just the same.

Went to the Everest zone, couldn't even find it as you can't see far enough (mountains just disappear rather than go into the distance)

I don't think this game was ready, even for early release, I expect it to be buggy but after 20 mins I put this down and unlikely to pick back up until there are some serious updates. Apart from being able to turn your head and see inside your cabin this isn't much better than a flight sim from Amiga days! Actually they had more depth!

Would be great to get my money back to buy an alternative but hope this improves.

developer_response.body: Hi, we're aware of the issues and have an update releasing by January 1st that will fix many of these issues.  Expect many updates to come over the coming weeks and months.  Regarding the terrain limitations and draw distance.  We're pushing over 1 million Tris based on any given area and the Quest simply cannot handle any more than that, this is why the draw distance has to be limited.  Having said that, we're constantly optimizing and will have updates that will improve performance in the weeks and months ahead.  Please stick with us, we'll show you how great Flight Unlimited will be.

reviews.review_description:
Firstly, no, I was not expecting MSFS2024. I read the reviews, looked at a few YouTube video reviews and decided to give it a go, against my better judgment considering the price. 

This thing is bad on so many levels. Not saying it won’t be decent at some point but definitely not ready for release, even a beta release. Never mind the graphics, the physics are completely wrong, this could have easily been corrected by someone with even basic flying experience.

Wish I hadn’t wasted 40$ on this…This feels like buying a bad IPO, we’re funding the development or new cars for these guys. Sorry

developer_response.body: I'm sorry you don't like the trajectory of the game.  We'll be updating extensively over the coming weeks and months.  We’re using a very sophisticated flight model, but each plane requires tweaks. Everything is modeled down to the individual actuators on the elevators. We’re going to give a raw visualization of the flight data in an update next week. Including lift on every surface, engine thrust, etc. We’re also going to update to allow the user to modify trim and sensitivity.

reviews.review_description:
Not a pilot but did R/C in my youth. I have figured out how to get off the ground, but I could really use a manual. 

Regards, Phil-8

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing. We have a full tutorial with missions releasing soon

reviews.review_description:
The game is early access, and some planes and maps are more polished than others.  There are already quite a few planes.
I look forward to watching this sim grow.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the comments, we just submitted a new update that unlocks the E2C Hawkeye and fixes a few bugs.  

The next major update will be the G1000 (Garmin) for visualization and flight planning.  Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: Wow what can I say, for an early release I was extremely impressed. This isn't a finished game and a reminder that the developers behind this are pouring their heart and soul into making this an amazing flight simulator. Well worth buying and one of my sons had a shot and really enjoyed flying about in different planes. Graphics although in early development are still really good and I am looking forward to the further developments that are coming.

Not bad for an early look. 

However the devs need to learn the difference between a Warhawk and a Mustang. 

Also the flight model could be improved. Better than some games but I'd appreciate improved accelerated and departure stall behavior.

developer_response.body: We appreciate the feedback.  We're working on opening up the flight model to user tweaks...it's extremely advanced. If you look at the tablet in the aircraft you'll see all of the raw data that we're analyzing in real time. Stay tuned for updates!

reviews.review_description:
First off, totally agreed on the rotor wing request, however we know the physics and aerodynamics are a rework from the ground, up. There. 

Now for the real stuff, I would absolutely call this a flying "experience" more than a sim. It's way to operate, easy to control, it's self-contained on the headset AND you made the cockpits look photo-realistic, getting clearer as you get closer to the instruments, and "oh, no, the scenery isn't like MSXYZ" - no kidding, you're not wearing a 5090 on your head, either folks. 

The Dev has literally walked the edge of the tech with gfx vs FPS and for THAT, on a Q3, is magical. This is by FAR the first flight app standalone that I'm excited to show my pilot friends. I purchased the original Flight Unlimited way back in the day, I thank you Devs, a nod to nostalgia, the original was awesome then, you have resurrected it with the utmost in entertainment! I check daily for your updates, wheels up, over, Over! ;)

developer_response.body: Thanks for the well thought out comments, we appreciate your support!

reviews.review_description: The brakes don't work to slow airspeed to land properly.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the comments. In 2-3 weeks we’re releasing map functionality and waypoints via a Garmin G1000. Modeler very closely to the real Garmin. Runway lights will be included soon as well

reviews.review_description: I first played this on my oculus go back in the oculus days and when I got the quest 2 was hoping I could play it on it but it was not  an option then.  Saw it coming now to quest 3 and 3s could not wait, sure it it no microsoft flight sim but works.  The closest you are gonna get to microsoft flight sim on stand alone vr.  Wish they did not still use the google map thingy but better then having a fake town.   Glad to see they  corrected the spelling of San Francisco, they spelled it wrong on the GO version.

...that it's very difficult to create a solid flight simulation for the Quest. And I really appreciate that you are trying.

I'm a RC model pilot and generally interested in aviation. I give you 3 stars for now and I won't ask for a refund to support you!

The new moveable panel for trim- and sensivity adjustments and more is a big improvement! Could you make the adjustment steps a bit smaller? Because I can't trim some planes totally neutral. And if I adjust the sliders the throttle decreases, that should be fixed.

A big issue is still the low resolution! In principle, the cockpits look good, but from the sitting position one can't read the gauges properly. But this is a core element of a simulation. How can you use a flight simulation if you can't read the numbers on the dashboard because they're very blurry?
A little higher resolution with Quest Games Optimizer doesn't work for long well, although CPU and GPU level are set to maximum. Especially if there're some objects in the scenery (For example San Francisco).
The game then hangs and the Quest doesn't react. Seems overloaded. Too little RAM? 8 GB is nothing... I don't know how to manage this issue.

Furthermore, AppSW is causing significant artifacts for example on the wing struts of the Cessna and the wings of the DR400 when they move on the ground. In the air it's better. But maybe this is the price one has to pay to get a decent frame rate.

The engines of the DC3 look like jet engines from the front, with a fan and the typical cone but with a propeller put on it's top although it has radial piston engines in reality.
And the turning fans of the 747 don't look good. Distorted and with artefacts. Make them static, less movement, less problems. Normally you can't see them in the game, I guess. I have to lean far out of the cockpit to even spot that, it was coincidence.

I wonder if the planes are all  the same scale. Could you please check this? In the hangar there is for example a big difference between the DR400 and the Cessna. The Cessna looks huge, the DR400 tiny like the Pitts. That is not the case in reality. And the control stick of the F-18 seems too large, too thick, compared for example to the A-10.

Are buildings the same scale? Some buildings (the brown looking) at the San Francisco Airport seem much too high, it's with doors, windows seem too large, too.

Is it possible, to make the entire scenery and the sky brighter? Both appear relatively dark, although the Quest's brightness is set to maximum.

Still a picture of a P-51 Mustang in the menu although you have a Curtiss P-40.

Could you add a quit button to the menu? If I want to close it over the meta button, it takes some long moments to do this. The quest is too busy.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comments...we're working on a multitude of fixes and updates, stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: Thought I'd give this a try and this has great potential and I'm excited to see how this develops. Would love some UK airports though. Nothing like flying an F18 in a spin and leaving your stomach on the floor. Keep up the great work

For a game that is still developing it’s doing amazing, yes sometimes the graphics bug out a little but the game play still feels nice. I hope that this becomes similar to Microsoft flight simulator in terms of there’s way more planes and you can fly wherever you want with detailed graphics through the entire map. If you’re looking for a game that feels like a flight sim I do recommend this but do understand it’s still in development so there will be flaws.

developer_response.body: We appreciate your support, and thanks for the comments. Expect many updates to come!

reviews.review_title: The first real flightsim for standalone
reviews.review_description: Excellent flightsim for standalone VR. It's fluid and you can fligh over Google map.   the cessna works relatively well; the Robin has a tendency to go up... some ideas for the developpers, has this is still an early access : being able to use only the joysticks to pilot as to keep the arms up all the time is difficult.  We would need also to be able to trim the plane.  More 3D buildings would be a plus, if the helmet allows. Could we preload a zone of Google 3d???

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comments. We’re working on adding trim controls. An update to use the joystick to control the plane is a good idea. Regarding buildings, the scenery alone is pushing over 1 million polys at any given time (ie: hills and mountains), and the Google buildings will kill the game completely. We’re hand modeling buildings in specific areas, and will work on adding more very soon

reviews.review_description: I've been flying for quite a long time on PC and others flying games but just on the VR quest. It is awesome! Right on guys

developer_response.body: Thank you! We have a major update releasing in 2-3 weeks that adds the Garmin G1000 to select cockpits with navigation and ILS

reviews.review_description: This is Early Access first and foremost, so don't come into this expecting a fully fleshed out MFS experience. What you will get is a well optimized, easy to use, and fun standalone flight sim. I'll definitely be putting more time into this, and I'm excited for more maps, planes, and activities! Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body: We appreciate your support and your comments.  This is very early access, and we're dedicated to improving the game with countless updates over the coming weeks and months.

reviews.review_description: Big disappointment you can control throttle , Brake , Flaps and start engine only the other buttons not working I think dawn of jets better than these game not worth it

developer_response.body: We have Throttle, Flaps, Brake, Gear, and Flight Stick currently for the aircraft...additional controls will be molded down the road

reviews.review_description:
Land graphics are horrible. 
VR controls are bad.
No tutorial for learning anything.
Push X to start jet, are you kidding me.
There are much better flight games that are more "flight sim" than this trash. This isn't worth $5.
I want a refund.
DO NOT BUY.

developer_response.body:
Update: 
Big news! The latest update (released today) adds full controls and startup procedure to the Cessna! The other planes will follow in the coming weeks!

The graphics are being pushed to the max. The scenery is using high definition satellite maps and accurate terrain and elevation maps. More buildings will come in a future update, but the Quest hardware isn’t capable of pushing any more polys than we already are.

This is early access and we’re releasing updates multiple times a week. A tutorial will be released in two weeks, but the flying is intuitive. The tablet in the cockpit shows all relevant data including physics values. 

As for pressing a button to start the plane, it’s tedious and difficult to include the full startup procedure without keyboard overlay.

reviews.review_description: So far, this is the best flight sim i have found. Hope they keep working on it because it is still very good at this stage.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing and thanks for your support.  Many more updates to come!

reviews.review_title: Please add and fix these things
reviews.review_description: It’s a very good flight simulator game. surprisingly you don’t have to do missions to get free flights! But please add the airbus a350 cause it’s my favourite airplane. And please make the flying a little bit easier cause it’s very advanced for new flyers. But honestly best flight simulator on vr so far in my opinion, and please add/fix these thing.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the comments. We’re working on updates and will definitely add the airbus a350 to the list

reviews.review_description:
Before I begin my review, I should probably mention that I got my copy of the game free from Reddit, and that I have no experience piloting planes irl or in any simulator so I can't really comment on how realistic the whole experience is.

Overall though, I had fun. It was nice flying around the environment and trying my best to land. I did successfully land after a while albeit missing the runway completely, but then I found that I couldn't move the plane anymore to take off again. I had no idea why.

The game environment isn't the best (the world is a flat earthers dream). I understand that there are limitations to standalone, but hopefully in the future you're able to implement some 3D elements (buildings/monuments/trees) atleast when on the ground. With regards to controls, I would have liked it if there were clear indications for when the brakes were engaged, if the landing gear was up or down, and the direction of the rudder, and I also noticed that flaps had no fine control, so for example on the Cessna it was either fully up or down in the cockpit.

It's still early days, but hopefully the game keeps being updated and made even better. I look forward to more locations and more planes (maybe passenger planes).

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback. We’re updating the game largely based on customer comments, expect updates soon with many of your comments addressed.

reviews.review_description: I played for 5 minutes and hated how it's full of glitches. It's a very buggy app with poor graphics even on the 3S. To make matters worse I can't find get a refund after trying 10 minutes after purchasing. I just want to delete and refund.

developer_response.body:
Please email us, we’re not aware of any glitches. Can you please update your review with details? If you can send us an endless with specifics that will be helpful. We’re working daily on updates: mail@tasappsystems.com

Thank you

reviews.review_description: This is one of the worst flight simulators they could have made.  Absolutely zero interaction with any aircraft functions except stick and throttle. The banking angles are so bad you loose all control of the aircraft. They need to hit the delete button and start from scratch!!!

developer_response.body:
Update: 
Big news! The latest update (released today) adds full controls and startup procedure to the Cessna! The other planes will follow in the coming weeks!

reviews.review_description: Loving it for relaxing during work breaks, if you want an app that's relaxing, chilled and realistic, seeing the grand canyon is incredible. The controls are pretty easy to get used to, but takes a bit more master. I'm looking forwards to seeing where this is going!

I'm really liking this flight sim, I'm seeing the vision the devs have in mind, it's still in early Access so expect bugs but nothing game breaking, the visuals are ok and is going to get an update in the future, I'm only expecting greatness in the near future, it's a great deal for the price, so grab it asap because the price will go up.

And I will recommended this game in it's current state so take Flight and get this nice hidden flight gem
